description: POST /v1/join — unauthenticated. Redeems a single-use invite code minted by an owner device via POST /v1/invites. P0-6 self-routing (v1-architecture.md §10.5): `space` is REQUIRED — the Worker routes directly to that space's SpaceHub (env.SPACE_HUB.getByName(space)) with zero KV lookup. Here the client derived `space` by decoding the connect code's embedded space_id locally (chars [1..10], lowercased); the self-host deep-link path sends the identical shape with `space` taken straight from the URL instead. Matches openapi.yaml #/paths/~1v1~1join/post.
decode_note: code = 'X' + space_id(10, uppercased) + secret(9) + 'Z'. Decoding 'XK7M3QZX2VTQ7T9M2K4NZ': chars[1..10] = 'K7M3QZX2VT' -> lowercased 'k7m3qzx2vt' == the space this device is joining (also sent explicitly as `space`, per the unified shape); chars[11..19] = 'Q7T9M2K4N' is the one-time secret the server looks up in the target SpaceHub's DeviceRegistry.invites (v1-architecture.md §10.5).

error_response_400_malformed_code.description: code fails the shape check (wrong length, missing/wrong X…Z anchors, or an out-of-alphabet character).

error_response_400_space_mismatch.description: code's embedded space_id does not match the routed `space` field — e.g. a code pasted from a different space's invite, or a typo in a manually-entered space. Caught as defense-in-depth after the Worker has already routed on `space` (v1-architecture.md §10.5 step 3).

error_response_404.description: Invite invalid, expired (>10 min), or already redeemed (single-use). The secret decoded from `code` was not found in the routed space's DeviceRegistry.invites.
